title: Cross-Area Journey
description: One file per journey at .spec/journeys/<slug>.json. A journey is THE use-case mechanism: a named user-visible flow, steps referencing requirement IDs in qualified <area>.<ID> form, in temporal order. Most journeys live inside one area (a 'login' flow over auth alone is a normal journey); some cross boundaries — same shape either way. Same overlay discipline as change manifests: references only, never content — the requirements stay in their area JSONs; delete every journey and the specs are complete. Captured naturally during elicitation: one story walked with the user = one journey. Documentation/review layer; no formal verification obligation (a joint-reachability witness across modules is a possible later extension).

name.description: Short flow name, e.g. 'Sign up and buy'. Unique across journeys.

description.description: One line: what the actor accomplishes end to end.

steps.description: Temporal order — this order IS the render order in readbacks. Happy-path step followed immediately by its unwanted counterparts.

ref.description: Qualified requirement reference, <area>.<ID> — same form as cross_refs.

note.description: Optional gloss for the step in journey context, e.g. 'payment declined counterpart'.
